synopsis: Kirk Lyons and others complained that Western Carolina Presbytery erred when it did not find a strong presumption of guilt against TE Craig Bulkeley in a 31-2 investigation, and violated due process by failing to interview the accusing parties. The Standing Judicial Commission sustained the complaint in part, finding that the Presbytery had denied the complainants due process by not allowing them to present evidence before finding no strong presumption of guilt.
